questions.question: What were some major problems faced by women in the 19th century?
questions.correct_answer: Child marriage, lack of education, and restrictions on widows
questions.explanation: In the nineteenth century many social practices limited women's rights and opportunities in society.

questions.question: Who were social reformers?
questions.correct_answer: People who worked to change unfair social practices
questions.explanation: Reformers challenged harmful traditions and promoted equality, education, and women's rights.

questions.question: Why was widow remarriage an important reform?
questions.correct_answer: It allowed widows to remarry and live with dignity
questions.explanation: Earlier widows were not allowed to remarry and faced social discrimination.

questions.question: Why was women's education important for social reform?
questions.correct_answer: Education helped women gain knowledge and independence
questions.explanation: Reformers believed education would improve women's status and opportunities in society.

questions.question: What is caste discrimination?
questions.correct_answer: Unfair treatment of people based on their caste
questions.explanation: Lower caste communities were often denied education, jobs, and social equality.

questions.question: How did reformers try to reduce caste inequality?
questions.correct_answer: By promoting education and equal rights for all communities
questions.explanation: Many reform movements aimed to challenge caste barriers and improve social equality.

questions.question: What role did print culture play in social reform?
questions.correct_answer: It helped spread reform ideas through books and newspapers
questions.explanation: Printed materials allowed reformers to reach a wider audience and create awareness about social issues.

questions.question: Why did some people oppose social reforms?
questions.correct_answer: They believed reforms threatened traditional customs
questions.explanation: Many conservative groups resisted changes to long-standing social practices.

questions.question: Why were social reform laws introduced?
questions.correct_answer: To legally support changes and protect people's rights
questions.explanation: Government laws helped reduce harmful practices and supported reform movements.

questions.question: What was the impact of social reform movements?
questions.correct_answer: They improved social equality and promoted education and justice
questions.explanation: Reform movements laid the foundation for modern ideas of equality and human rights in India.

notes.title: Status of Women in the 19th Century
notes.content: Women had limited rights and faced practices such as child marriage and restrictions on education.

notes.title: Role of Reformers
notes.content: Social reformers worked to change unfair traditions and promote equality in society.

notes.title: Women's Education
notes.content: Education for women was promoted by reformers to improve their social and economic status.

notes.title: Caste Inequality
notes.content: The caste system created social hierarchy and discrimination against lower caste communities.

notes.title: Print Culture and Reform
notes.content: Newspapers and books helped spread ideas about social reform and encouraged public debate.

facts.fact: Social reform movements in the 19th century aimed to improve women's rights and reduce caste discrimination.
Education played an important role in empowering women and marginalized communities.
Print media helped reformers spread awareness about social problems.
Many social reforms eventually led to new laws that protected people's rights.
